statement: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget.
The system shall allow the IFA to monitor the budget balance and ensure proper financial management.
The system shall alert administrators to budget violations.
The system shall allow the IFA to audit team budgets and set regulatory policies.
The system shall allow the financial administrator to view individual transactions.
The system shall allow the referee to insert game events in real time.
The system shall allow the referee to use a mobile phone to record game events in real time.
The system shall allow only the main referee or the side referee to insert game events.
The system shall allow all users to view the complete aggregated data.
The system shall allow guests to view aggregated final data.
The system shall allow the aggregation and export of seasonal statistics such as red cards and goals per team for distribution to external parties such as journalists or press.
The system shall allow unregistered users to access game-related data such as results, goals, and red cards.
The system shall allow team managers and team administrators to access financial data for offline analysis.
The system shall make financial data accessible to all members within the IFA and the finance department.
The system shall restrict budget transaction entry to individual teams, with each team only able to access and modify their own data.
The system shall allow the IFA to view the entire monetary information.
The system shall allow IFA to monitor schedules, send alerts, and request adjustment details, but shall not permit IFA to perform direct operations on the scheduling data.
The system shall have the ability to define policies for scheduling games.
The system shall provide guidance for allocating referees to games based on defined policies.
The system shall ensure that the same referee is not assigned to multiple games involving the same teams.
The system shall enable referees to set their availability preferences.
The system shall not allow teams to modify or influence the schedule once it is set for the season.
The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
The system shall allow manual override of scheduled results under certain circumstances.
The system shall send push email notifications to fans when their favorite players post new messages.
The system shall allow fans to register for notifications about specific games and send them notifications when related updates occur.
The system shall include all functionality within the application or website.
The system shall support multiple interfaces for different user roles or purposes.
The system shall allow only referees to update game data.
The system shall allow users to specify alerts, such as a reminder five minutes before the game starts.
The system shall support concurrent access by 50,000 users with fast response times.
The system shall enable real-time communication between referees and the application during games.
The system shall be developed and maintained by an external company.
The system shall facilitate communication between the software development team and the IFA through the organization's IT Department.
The system shall have all storage and computing resources managed by an external cloud service provider after deployment.
The system shall be adjustable to comply with local regulations and local review requirements.
The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent unauthorized access or data leakage.
The system shall allow players to update their social network page.
The system shall support team registration, league setup, season configuration, referee registration, and fan registration as core functionalities.
The system shall support budgeting and scheduling functionalities in the second phase.
The system shall allow the IFA representative administration role to manage budgeting, scheduling, and all related activities.
The system shall allow team managers or owners to register their team via the internet so that the team can be identified in the system.
The system shall allow team managers or owners to submit a new team registration with valid documents, which can be approved by the IFA.
The system shall retain all past scheduling archives.
The system shall allow the IFA to define leagues, define seasons, appoint or register referees, and set policies for budgets and scheduling.
The system shall allow an IFA representative to invite a referee to join the system.
The system shall allow the team to enter player details into the system.
The system shall allow the IFA to audit the referee and player registration process.
The system shall maintain a complete audit log of every action performed by IFA administrators.
The system shall provide a financial summary dashboard giving the IFA a quick view of key budget metrics across all teams.
The system shall provide a public API for journalists to programmatically access game data in a later version.
The system shall allow referees to enter match events into the application in real time during the match.
The system shall display the current league standings publicly to all users.
The system shall allow fans to compare player statistics side by side.
